# Claude credential protocol — long-lived OAuth token (Pro/Max)

The DeepResearch loop's 6 Claude agents all bind credential **`default`**. The **canonical** auth is a
**long-lived OAuth token tied to your Claude.ai Pro/Max subscription**, generated by `claude setup-token`.
This is the durable fix for the recurring ~5‑hour interactive-login expiry that crashed earlier runs: a
`setup-token` token is long-lived (~1 year), so the loop authenticates unattended on your subscription
without re-login.

## Why this method (vs the alternatives)
| Method | Expires? | Billing | Use |
|---|---|---|---|
| **`setup-token` long-lived OAuth** (canonical) | ~1 year | **Claude.ai Pro/Max subscription** | **default** |
| Interactive `credentials claude login` | ~5 h access token | subscription | avoid — short-lived, caused the crashes |
| `--api-key` (Console) | no (until rotated) | API/Console credits | alt, if you prefer Console billing |
| third-party proxy (`ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`+`AUTH_TOKEN`) | n/a | proxy account balance | **not used** — see the hard rule below |

## Set it (operator runs; the token is a secret — never paste it to an agent/transcript)
```bash
P=/home/linfeng/houmao_DeepScientist
claude setup-token                       # interactive (browser/device flow); prints a long-lived token
houmao-mgr project --project-dir "$P" credentials claude set --name default \
    --oauth-token '<TOKEN>' \
    --clear-api-key --clear-auth-token --clear-config-dir   # bundle carries ONLY CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
```
Verify (no secret printed):
```bash
houmao-mgr project --project-dir "$P" credentials claude get --name default
# expect: CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN present:true ; no ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL / ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN in the bundle
```

## HARD RULE — do not let a proxy shadow the token
If `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` **and** `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` are set in the **launch environment**, Claude Code
uses that third-party endpoint and **ignores** the OAuth token (this is exactly what silently happened in an
earlier run via `yunwuapi.com`, and a depleted proxy balance — not the subscription — blocked the loop).
Therefore **launch agents from a shell with those unset**:
```bash
unset ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
# or per-launch:  env -u ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL -u ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN -u ANTHROPIC_API_KEY houmao-mgr ... agents launch ...
```
Confirm the live posture after launch:
```bash
S=$(tmux ls | grep -oE 'HOUMAO-orchestrator-[0-9]+' | head -1)
tr '\0' '\n' < /proc/$(tmux list-panes -t "$S" -F '#{pane_pid}')/environ | grep -i ANTHROPIC
# should show NO ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL / ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN — the agent is on the OAuth token
```

## Credential changes require a fresh relaunch
Claude Code caches the credential into each agent **home** at launch and a bare `agents single … relaunch`
**reuses** that home (stale token). After any credential change (new `setup-token`, switch to API key, etc.):
stop the agents and **launch fresh** so the new credential is read. (If you only refresh the central bundle,
the running agents keep the old token until relaunched.)

## Renewal
`setup-token` tokens are long-lived but not eternal (~1 year). When the orchestrator/agents eventually show a
genuine `401 Invalid authentication credentials` (not a quota/balance 403), re-run `claude setup-token` +
the `credentials claude set` above, then relaunch fresh.
